title: GARP/SRS Receipt Envelope
description: Minimal envelope-level schema for a GARP/SRS conformance receipt. This schema validates receipt envelope FORM only: that the outer envelope carries the structural fields a verifier needs to route and version a receipt. It does NOT validate the receipt body, nor truth, correctness, compliance, security, performance, or external adoption. The body is intentionally treated as opaque at this layer and is the responsibility of a future body_kind-specific schema.
$comment: Stage C2 artifact. Envelope-level only. No body_kind is registered by this schema beyond the envelope vocabulary.

schema_version.description: Version marker of the envelope schema this receipt declares conformance to. For this artifact the expected value is "0.1".

receipt_kind.description: Stable identifier for the kind of receipt carried by this envelope. The envelope schema does not constrain the set of valid kinds; kind-specific meaning and constraints are defined elsewhere.

body_kind.description: Stable identifier selecting the schema/vocabulary that governs the body. This is the documented extension point (see docs/BODY_KIND_EXTENSION_RULES.md). No body_kind is registered by this envelope schema; it only requires that one be declared.

body.description: Receipt payload. Treated as opaque at the envelope layer: the envelope schema asserts only that a body object is present, not its internal shape. Body contents are validated by the body_kind-specific schema, which is out of scope for this artifact.

receipt_id.description: Optional opaque identifier for this receipt instance. Form only; the envelope does not assert uniqueness or provenance.

issued_at.description: Optional issuance timestamp in RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 date-time form. Form only; the envelope does not assert the timestamp is accurate or trusted.

ext.description: Controlled extension namespace. Forward-compatible carrier for additional envelope-level fields that are not yet standardized. Kept separate so the top-level envelope can stay strict while still allowing controlled extension.
